title: Minimal Sets and Properties of Feral Pseudoholomorphic Curves
speaker: Joel Fish
abstract: I will discuss some current joint work with Helmut Hofer, in which we define and establish properties of a new class of pseudoholomorhic curves (feral J-curves) to study certain divergence free flows in dimension three. In particular, we show that if H is a smooth, proper, Hamiltonian on R^4, then no non-empty regular energy level of H is minimal. That is, the flow of the associated Hamiltonian vector field has a trajectory which is not dense.

proof_stages.label: Dynamical theorem and minimal-flow problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk states the main theorem that a compact regular Hamiltonian energy level in standard R4 is not minimal, defines minimality, and situates the result among Seifert's periodic-orbit problem and Gottschalk's minimal-flow question on S3.

proof_stages.label: Contact neck-stretching benchmark
proof_stages.summary: In the tight contact S3 case, Gromov's degree-one pseudoholomorphic spheres in CP2 survive neck stretching, automatic finite-energy bounds hold, and limiting punctured curves become asymptotic to Reeb orbit cylinders, yielding a periodic orbit.

proof_stages.label: Framed hypersurfaces and loss of energy bounds
proof_stages.summary: The attempted extension from contact-type hypersurfaces to arbitrary closed hypersurfaces replaces the contact form by a framed Hamiltonian structure, but known aperiodic Hamiltonian examples force the diagnosis that the da wedge lambda part of the local area can become unbounded even while the omega-energy remains controlled.

proof_stages.label: Uniform area bounds near extrema
proof_stages.summary: The first key theorem proves that for curves with bounded omega-energy, the full area in any fixed-height region above an absolute minimum, and similarly below a maximum, is uniformly bounded independently of the particular curve.

proof_stages.label: Target-local and exhaustive Gromov compactness
proof_stages.summary: The near-minimum area estimates, together with genus bounds, allow target-local Gromov compactness to be applied after translating the minima, and a diagonal exhaustive compactness argument extracts a proper limiting pseudoholomorphic curve running out the positive end.

proof_stages.label: Finite generalized punctures and feral curves
proof_stages.summary: Because lack of total energy bounds could create infinitely many ends or worse limiting domains, the argument backs up to the neck-stretching sequence and uses capped-off components with threshold omega-energy to bound the number of generalized punctures, leading to the definition of a feral curve as a proper curve with finite omega-energy, genus, punctures, and finite marked/nodal/component data.

proof_stages.label: Analytic end control and proper invariant limit set
proof_stages.summary: The remaining analysis establishes connected local area bounds, immersion and curvature control, and vertical asymptotics for feral ends; these properties yield closed invariant limit sets, and a workhorse intersection argument shows the limit set is not the whole energy level.

examples.name: Sheeted wrapping causing local area blow-up
examples.note: The picture of curves wrapping in longer and longer sheets through fixed slices is presented as the mechanism of energy loss and leads to the key focus on what happens near an absolute minimum.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker traces the sheeting/wrapping mechanism, explaining how loops grow, tangent planes become vertical, and local area/energy blows up.

examples.name: Infinitely holed disk domain
examples.note: The possible limiting domain as a disk minus infinitely many open disks illustrates why the limit need not be an ordinary punctured Riemann surface without further topological control.
examples.engagement_reason: The infinitely holed disk domain is considered as a possible limiting pathology and then reasoned against using caps and omega-energy thresholds.

passes.reason: a disk with infinitely many holes is an infinite-type limiting domain used to defeat the punctured-surface picture
